title: iCrunchy Kenyan Shrimp Griddles
description: A pungent, crumbly, adventurous Kenyan dish featuring shrimp stock and double-rise bread, perfect for an iron teppan griddle.

ingredients.name: shrimp stock
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought, without onions or garlic

instructions: Preheat the iron teppan griddle over medium-high heat to 400°F (200°C).
Add 1 tablespoon of coconut oil to the preheated griddle and swirl to coat.
Add the cubed double-rise bread to the griddle and toast until lightly browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
In a small bowl, mix together the shrimp stock, curry powder, and paprika.
Add the shrimp to the griddle and cook until they start to turn pink, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Pour the shrimp stock mixture over the shrimp and cook until the liquid is almost fully absorbed, about 2-3 minutes more.
Add the toasted bread back to the griddle, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in the lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and green chili flakes.
Serve the iCrunchy Kenyan Shrimp Griddles hot, garnished with lemon zest if desired.
Let the griddle rest for 2-3 minutes before wiping clean with a damp cloth.
